description:
A developer just cloned a repository which was placed at:

  /home/user/my_project

  The directory already contains a typical “src / docs / tests” layout.  
  To help other team-members get a quick overview, create a manifest log that lists **every regular file** in the project tree (i.e., everything that is *not* a directory, symlink, or special file).

  Your goal:
    1. From inside /home/user/my_project produce a **new text file** called:
         /home/user/my_project/file_manifest.log
    2. The file must contain:
         • One entry per line.  
         • Entries are the *relative* paths that would be printed by the command “find . -type f”, i.e. each path must start with “./”.  
         • The list must be sorted in pure lexicographical order (the same order “sort” with no options would output).  
         • After the very last filename add a final line summarising the count of files, in the exact form  
           TOTAL=<number>  
           where <number> is the integer count of lines that precede this summary line.
    3. When you are finished, /home/user/my_project/file_manifest.log should look conceptually like:

         ./example1
         ./example2
         …
         TOTAL=N

       (but, of course, with the real project paths and correct N).

  Notes / constraints:
    • Use only standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities already present in a minimal environment (e.g. find, sort, wc, etc.).  
    • A solution that executes in **two or three commands** from the terminal is sufficient.  
    • The automated test will:
        – Confirm that file_manifest.log exists at the required path.  
        – Verify its exact byte-for-byte contents (names, order, “TOTAL=” line, trailing newline if any).  
    • Do not modify, move, or delete any existing project files; only create the new log file.
truth:
Initial directory structure provided before the task starts:

  /home/user/my_project/
  ├── README.md
  ├── docs
  │   └── usage.txt
  ├── src
  │   ├── app.py
  │   └── helpers.py
  └── tests
      ├── test_app.py
      └── test_helpers.py

  Six regular files exist:

    ./README.md
    ./docs/usage.txt
    ./src/app.py
    ./src/helpers.py
    ./tests/test_app.py
    ./tests/test_helpers.py

  Therefore the correct manifest file, once the agent finishes, **must** be exactly:

    ./README.md
    ./docs/usage.txt
    ./src/app.py
    ./src/helpers.py
    ./tests/test_app.py
    ./tests/test_helpers.py
    TOTAL=6

  Path and name required:

    /home/user/my_project/file_manifest.log

  The automated checker will read that file and compare against the text block above (including the trailing newline that the shell usually appends when redirecting output).
